properties.name: Russia — Ukraine
properties.nuclear_risk: HIGH — Russia nuclear doctrine, battlefield tactical weapons
properties.summary: Largest interstate war in Europe since WWII. Front line runs hundreds of miles across eastern/southern Ukraine. Fortified with trenches, minefields, drones, long-range missile strikes. No settlement as of 2026.

properties.name: Israel — Gaza / Lebanon / Syria / Iran
properties.casualties_estimate: Gaza: 45,000+ killed; Lebanon front: thousands; direct Iran exchange 2025
properties.nuclear_risk: MEDIUM — Israel undeclared deterrent; Iran nuclear program
properties.summary: Hamas attack Oct 7 2023 triggered Gaza war. Hezbollah front in Lebanon. Israel-Iran direct exchange 2025 involving US. Syrian frontier active. Most volatile border cluster on earth.

properties.name: North Korea — South Korea (DMZ)
properties.status: FROZEN — Armistice since 1953; no peace treaty
properties.casualties_estimate: Technical state of war; periodic incidents, missile tests
properties.nuclear_risk: CRITICAL — North Korea operational nuclear arsenal, ICBM capability
properties.summary: 160-mile strip. Most heavily fortified border on earth. Landmines, troops, surveillance. Korean War armistice 1953 — no peace treaty. Both sides don't recognize each other. Nuclear-armed standoff.

properties.name: India — Pakistan (Line of Control, Kashmir)
properties.status: ESCALATED — Operation Sindoor May 2025; ceasefire fragile
properties.casualties_estimate: Operation Sindoor 2025: 26 civilians killed (Pahalgam); cross-border strikes exchanged
properties.nuclear_risk: CRITICAL — Two nuclear states, geographic proximity, no stable second-strike doctrine
properties.summary: Most militarized nuclear border. 1947 partition created Line of Control in Kashmir. Operation Sindoor May 2025: India struck Pakistan after militant attack. Visible from orbit at night. Repeated wars.

properties.name: Sudan — Chad (Darfur Corridor)
properties.status: ACTIVE — Sudan civil war Apr 2023; 10M+ displaced
properties.casualties_estimate: 100,000+ killed since Apr 2023; one of world's deadliest current conflicts
properties.summary: Sudan civil war since April 2023 between Sudanese Armed Forces and RSF paramilitary. Largest displacement corridor on earth — 10M+ across Chad border. Mass atrocities near Darfur. Aid routes targeted.

properties.name: Afghanistan — Pakistan (Durand Line)
properties.status: VOLATILE — Taliban-Pakistan clashes; Torkham crossing closures
properties.casualties_estimate: Ongoing TTP attacks; Afghan cross-border airstrikes into Pakistan 2023-2025
properties.nuclear_risk: LOW-MEDIUM — Pakistan nuclear state; risk of escalation
properties.summary: Durand Line 1893 — Afghanistan never recognized it. Taliban retook Afghanistan 2021. Relations with Pakistan soured: cross-border clashes, airstrikes, Torkham crossing closures. Militant corridor for smuggling and sudden firefights.

properties.name: Yemen — Saudi Arabia
properties.status: FRAGILE TRUCE — 2022 UN truce holds; Houthis still control north Yemen
properties.casualties_estimate: 377,000+ total Yemen war deaths (UNDP 2021); ongoing Houthi missile/drone attacks
properties.summary: Saudi Arabia entered Yemen civil war 2015. Houthi movement controls north. 1100-mile mountainous border. UN truce 2022 reduced heaviest fighting but fragile. Houthis extend operations to Red Sea cable cutting and commercial shipping attacks.

properties.name: Mexico — United States
properties.status: ONGOING — Cartel wars; US security priority 2026
properties.casualties_estimate: Migrants die from heat/exposure; cartel turf wars make border cities among hemisphere's most violent
properties.summary: Nearly 2,000 miles; most legal crossings globally. Danger = lethal human smuggling, drug trafficking, cartel violence. Remote desert crossings kill migrants. Cartel turf wars on Mexican side. US renewed security focus 2026.

properties.name: India — Bangladesh
properties.status: ONGOING — Routine killings; undocumented migration; smuggling
properties.casualties_estimate: Hundreds killed by India BSF annually; one of world's most porous borders
properties.summary: 2,500 miles — one of world's longest. Threads through villages, rivers, farmland. Major smuggling/migration route. India BSF criticized for killings of crossers. Porousness + routine violence = structural danger.

properties.name: Armenia — Azerbaijan (Nagorno-Karabakh)
properties.status: CAUTIOUSLY COOLING — White House peace initialed Aug 2025; unsigned as of 2026
properties.casualties_estimate: 2020 war: 6,000+ killed. 2023: Azerbaijan retook Karabakh; 100,000 Armenians fled
properties.summary: Three decades of violent conflict. 2020 war + 2023 offensive: Azerbaijan retook Nagorno-Karabakh, 100,000 ethnic Armenians fled. Aug 2025 White House peace initialed. Unsigned, fragile — but rare border on this list potentially cooling.
